questions.q: On which day of the Kurukshetra war did Bhishma fall?
questions.explanation: Bhishma fell on the tenth day, ending his command of the Kaurava army.

questions.q: Who stood in front of Arjuna's attack so that Bhishma would not strike back?
questions.explanation: Arjuna attacked from behind Shikhandi, against whom Bhishma would not lift his bow.

questions.q: What kind of bed held Bhishma after he fell from his chariot?
questions.explanation: The arrows piercing his body held him above the ground in a hero's bed.

questions.q: Who gave Bhishma a pillow of three arrows for his head?
questions.explanation: When royal pillows were refused, Arjuna propped the grandsire's head on three arrows.

questions.q: How was Bhishma's thirst quenched as he lay fallen?
options: Duryodhana brought Ganga water in a golden cup
Arjuna shot an arrow into the earth and a spring rose up
Krishna summoned a rain cloud over the field
A passing sage offered water from his vessel
questions.explanation: Arjuna pierced the earth with an arrow, and a pure spring rose to Bhishma's lips.

questions.q: Whose waters were said to rise from the earth to quench the fallen Bhishma?
questions.explanation: The water that rose was said to be Ganga herself, come to soothe her son.

questions.q: What rare power allowed Bhishma to delay his own death?
options: A jewel that healed every wound
Amrita granted to him by the gods
The boon to die only when he chose
A blessing of invincibility from Shiva
questions.explanation: Shantanu's grateful boon let Bhishma choose the very hour of his death.

questions.q: For what turning of the sun did Bhishma wait before giving up his life?
questions.explanation: Bhishma held on until the Uttarayana, the sun's auspicious northern course.

questions.q: How many days did Bhishma command the Kaurava army before he fell?
questions.explanation: Bhishma led the Kaurava host for the war's first ten days.

questions.q: Whose arrows finally bore Bhishma off his chariot?
questions.explanation: Bhishma said Shikhandi's shafts touched him lightly, but Arjuna's bore him down.

questions.q: Why would Bhishma not loose a single arrow at Shikhandi?
options: He had sworn never to fight Panchala princes
He feared the curse Amba had laid on him
He would not fight one who had been born a woman
questions.explanation: Bhishma refused to fight Shikhandi, who had been born a woman as Amba reborn.

questions.q: Who granted Bhishma the boon of choosing the hour of his own death?
questions.explanation: Shantanu granted his son death-at-will in gratitude for his terrible vow.

questions.q: Why did Bhishma refuse the soft pillows that kings rushed to bring him?
options: He had vowed never to accept royal gifts
His wounds made any touch unbearable
They did not befit a warrior on a bed of arrows
He wished to keep his head upon the earth
questions.explanation: Bhishma said soft cushions did not suit a kshatriya lying on a bed of arrows.

questions.q: Why did Bhishma's body never touch the ground after his fall?
options: Divine hands held him above the field
The dense arrows through him held him aloft
The gods spread a celestial sheet beneath him
questions.explanation: So many shafts pierced him that his body rested on arrows, clear of the earth.

questions.q: How long is Bhishma traditionally said to have lain on the bed of arrows before dying?
questions.explanation: Tradition counts fifty-eight nights between his fall and his death after the solstice.

questions.q: What did Bhishma teach Yudhishthira at length from the bed of arrows after the war?
options: The duties of kings and the subtleties of dharma
The secrets of the celestial weapons
The full history of the Kuru ancestors
The science of archery and formations
questions.explanation: From the arrow bed Bhishma taught Yudhishthira rajadharma and the subtleties of dharma in the Shanti Parva.

questions.q: Which famous hymn of a thousand names did Bhishma impart before his death?
questions.explanation: Bhishma imparted the Vishnu Sahasranama, the thousand names of Vishnu, in the Anushasana Parva.

questions.q: Which warrior visited the fallen Bhishma and was urged to join the Pandavas as Kunti's son?
questions.explanation: Karna came by night; Bhishma called him Kunti's son and urged peace, then blessed him.

questions.q: Who visited Bhishma's tent by night, before his final day of battle, to ask how he could be defeated?
questions.explanation: The Pandavas, with Krishna, asked Bhishma himself how he might be overcome, and he told them.

questions.q: What happened on the battlefield in the moments after Bhishma fell?
options: The Pandavas pressed the attack to rout the Kauravas
Night fell suddenly by Krishna's illusion
Fighting ceased and warriors of both sides gathered round him
Duryodhana ordered an immediate retreat to camp
questions.explanation: Both armies laid down arms and gathered in grief around the fallen grandsire.

questions.q: Why did Bhishma wait for the Uttarayana to release his life?
options: He hoped the war might end before then
He was bound by a promise to Duryodhana
His wounds needed that long to overcome his boon
Dying in the sun's northern course is held supremely auspicious
questions.explanation: Death during the sun's northern course is held to lead to the highest realms.

questions.q: After the war, who brought the grieving Yudhishthira to Bhishma to receive his teachings?
questions.explanation: Krishna persuaded the grieving Yudhishthira and led him to Bhishma for instruction.

questions.q: What daily toll did Bhishma vow to take as Kaurava commander?
options: Ten thousand warriors slain each day
A hundred chariots burned each day
questions.explanation: Bhishma promised Duryodhana he would slay ten thousand warriors every day.

questions.q: Earlier in the war, when Krishna broke his own vow and rushed at Bhishma, what did he brandish in the most common retelling?
options: His whip alone, raised like a sword
The conch Panchajanya as a club
A chariot wheel snatched from the field
The mace of a fallen soldier
questions.explanation: Most retellings show Krishna charging Bhishma with a chariot wheel, though some versions name a whip or the discus.

questions.q: On which date is Bhishma's death traditionally commemorated?
options: The fifth of Shravana's bright fortnight
The eighth of Magha's bright fortnight
questions.explanation: Bhishma Ashtami, the eighth of Magha's bright fortnight, marks his chosen death after the Uttarayana began.

questions.q: What fell from the sky when the grandsire was struck down?
options: A sudden eclipse darkened the field
A rain of blood and dust
questions.explanation: The gods showered celestial flowers on the grandsire as he fell.

questions.q: What offer of Duryodhana's did the fallen Bhishma turn away?
options: Skilled physicians to draw out the arrows
A royal litter to carry him to the city
A guard of a thousand chariots
questions.explanation: Bhishma dismissed Duryodhana's surgeons, choosing to burn with the arrows as a warrior should.

questions.q: As whose incarnation did Bhishma return to heaven when he finally died?
questions.explanation: Bhishma was the Vasu Dyu reborn under Vashishtha's curse, and returned to the Vasus at death.

questions.q: Which weapon did Arjuna invoke to pierce the earth and bring up water for Bhishma?
questions.explanation: Arjuna invoked the Parjanya weapon to split the earth and raise the pure spring.

questions.q: What last counsel did Bhishma give Duryodhana from the bed of arrows?
options: To fight on under Drona's command
To end the enmity and make peace with the Pandavas
To guard Karna above all others
To send for reinforcements from Gandhara
questions.explanation: Bhishma begged Duryodhana to let the enmity end with his fall and make peace with the Pandavas.
